architecture.summary: A deterministic algebraic framework for transformer language models that constructs σ-regularized, nonsingular matrices for Q, K, V, and output weights, enabling attention and decoding to be computed in closed form without iterative optimization. Weight matrices are determined algebraically from paired input–output examples via direct matrix inversion, eliminating stochastic gradient descent and replacing iterative back propagation with finite algebraic equilibrium.

biblio.title: Algebraic Cekirge Method for Deterministic and Energy-efficient Transformer Language Models

claims.statement: The deterministic algebraic solution reproduces the predictive behavior of gradient descent while reducing computation time by more than sixty-fold
σ-regularization guarantees matrix invertibility and numerical stability
Attention and decoding can be computed in closed form without iterative stochastic gradient descent

The framework unifies Four-Cluster Deterministic Map, Frozen-Library Forward Training, σ-Matrix Fast Learning, and Inverse Deterministic Energy-Saving Training (IDEST) into a closed, energy-saving learning process
Deterministic algebraic decoding achieves comparable accuracy to gradient descent while operating with markedly lower computational energy
The model's internal mappings preserve identity under small deterministic perturbations (ε) but lose interpretability under stochastic noise
